interfaces.description:
Structured cell reference — A1-compatible. The field is always the column's stable name (never its letter) so the ref survives reorder.

  • row — 1-indexed display position. Ignored when sameRow.
  • absRow — row locked (matches $1 / $A$1; won't shift under fill).
  • absField — column locked (matches $A / $A$1; won't shift under fill).
  • sameRow — implicit current-row ref. Surface [field], storage REF(COLUMN("field")) (no ROW arg). Resolves against the host cell's row at evaluation time, so the same formula can be placed on every row without per-row rewriting. Mutually exclusive with absRow.
interfaces.rawdescription:
Structured cell reference — A1-compatible. The `field` is always the
column's stable name (never its letter) so the ref survives reorder.
  - `row`      — 1-indexed display position. Ignored when `sameRow`.
  - `absRow`   — row locked (matches `$1` / `$A$1`; won't shift under fill).
  - `absField` — column locked (matches `$A` / `$A$1`; won't shift under fill).
  - `sameRow`  — implicit current-row ref. Surface `[field]`, storage
                 `REF(COLUMN("field"))` (no ROW arg). Resolves against
                 the host cell's row at evaluation time, so the same
                 formula can be placed on every row without per-row
                 rewriting. Mutually exclusive with `absRow`.

properties.name: measuredRowHeights
properties.description:
Measured extra heights keyed by string rowKey (usually rowId). Backed by a Map, but every write bumps measureVersion — a signal — so VerticalVirtualScrollEngine's layout effect re-runs and rebuilds offsets whenever a detail row is (re)measured.

Why a counter and not a signal<Map> : the Map mutates in place for O(1) writes on a per-row basis; a signal would force Set.from(...) or a new Map per measurement, which is allocation pressure for nothing — the consumer only cares "did anything change", not "what changed where". The counter answers that question for free.

Backed by a Map, but every write bumps `measureVersion` — a signal —

for O(1) writes on a per-row basis; a signal<Map> would force
`Set.from(...)` or a new Map per measurement, which is allocation
pressure for nothing — the consumer only cares "did anything change",
not "what changed where". The counter answers that question for free.

methods.name: promoteFromPending
methods.rawdescription:
Called by the measurement zone once a hidden detail row has reported
its height via ResizeObserver. Atomically removes the id from
`pendingExpansion` and adds it to `expandedRowIds` — the visible body
template now mounts the detail row, and because the height was already
cached via `measureRow` just before, the virtual-scroll engine
rebuilds offsets with the correct value on the FIRST visible render.

Safe to call when the row was already cancelled (the pending check
exits early so we don't accidentally re-expand a row the user just
collapsed mid-measurement).

methods.name: syncFromSource
methods.rawdescription:
Reconciles the engine against `sourceData()`: any cell whose column
declares `allowFormula: true` and whose value is a `=…` string is
registered (or updated). Cells previously registered but no longer
present (row removed, value changed to a literal) are dropped.

Designed to be called from a grid-level effect tracking
`(formulas, sourceData, columnDefMap)` so that formulas baked into
the initial dataset are evaluated on first render — without it, the
engine would only learn about a formula after the user committed an
inline edit on it.

The reconciliation is incremental: identical formula strings short-
circuit (no re-parse, no re-eval) so the call is cheap on stable data.

properties.name: pendingExpansion
properties.description:
Two-phase expansion: when the user toggles a row to expand, the id is first added to pendingExpansion. A hidden measurement zone renders the detail row off-screen, its ResizeObserver measures the height, and the engine then PROMOTES the row from pendingExpansion into expandedRowIds — at which point the visible body template mounts the detail row with its height already cached, so the vertical virtual-scroll engine rebuilds offsets with the correct value on the very first visible render. No layout shift, no scrollbar bounce.

isRowExpanded(id) returns true ONLY once the row is in expandedRowIds (i.e. promoted) so the visible template never renders a detail row twice (once in the measurement zone, once in the layout).

`expandedRowIds` — at which point the visible body template mounts the

Route a cell-edit commit through the formula engine when appropriate:
 - `allowFormula` column + new value starts with `=` → register formula.
 - `allowFormula` column + old value was a formula but new one isn't

 - Any cell change → invalidate dependents so their cached value
   refreshes (no-op when the engine has zero dependents on this cell).

injectables.description:
Custom vertical virtualization that replaces cdk-virtual-scroll-viewport.

Why custom: CDK's strategy positions visible rows via transform: translateY on the content wrapper. transform creates a containing block that traps any position: sticky descendant, which breaks sticky pinned cells inside data rows. We replace the transform with a height-based top-spacer so the scroll container stays the sticky containing block.

The engine is fully signal-driven — two reactive effects run independently:

  • Layout effect: rebuilds the cumulative offset cache whenever the row layout (count, density, expanded set) changes. Cheap O(n) write.
  • Range effect: re-computes the visible window whenever scrollTop / viewportHeight / totalRowsHeight changes. O(log n) binary search.

Detail-row heights come from a single source of truth: the off-screen pre-measurement performed by <ad-grid-measurement-zone> writes into ExpandableRowEngine.measuredRowHeights (id-keyed). The visible-layout <ad-grid-detail-row> keeps that cache fresh via its own ResizeObserver. The engine NEVER observes row DOM directly — an earlier attempt with an index-keyed map produced scroll bounce because virtualization slides recycle DOM across indices, so a freshly observed element fires RO with the NEW index but reflects the OLD row's height, producing spurious deltas. Browser-level scroll anchoring (CSS overflow-anchor: auto on the viewport

  • each row) preserves the visible content's pixel position when the top spacer changes — no JS scroll compensation is needed.
injectables.rawdescription:
Custom vertical virtualization that replaces `cdk-virtual-scroll-viewport`.

Why custom: CDK's strategy positions visible rows via `transform: translateY`
on the content wrapper. `transform` creates a containing block that traps
any `position: sticky` descendant, which breaks sticky pinned cells inside
data rows. We replace the transform with a height-based top-spacer so the
scroll container stays the sticky containing block.

The engine is fully signal-driven — two reactive effects run independently:
- **Layout effect**: rebuilds the cumulative offset cache whenever the row
  layout (count, density, expanded set) changes. Cheap O(n) write.
- **Range effect**: re-computes the visible window whenever scrollTop /
  viewportHeight / totalRowsHeight changes. O(log n) binary search.

Detail-row heights come from a single source of truth: the off-screen
pre-measurement performed by `<ad-grid-measurement-zone>` writes into
`ExpandableRowEngine.measuredRowHeights` (id-keyed). The visible-layout
`<ad-grid-detail-row>` keeps that cache fresh via its own `ResizeObserver`.
The engine NEVER observes row DOM directly — an earlier attempt with an
index-keyed map produced scroll bounce because virtualization slides
recycle DOM across indices, so a freshly observed element fires RO with the
NEW index but reflects the OLD row's height, producing spurious deltas.
Browser-level scroll anchoring (CSS `overflow-anchor: auto` on the viewport
+ each row) preserves the visible content's pixel position when the top
spacer changes — no JS scroll compensation is needed.

components.description:
Hidden off-screen zone that PRE-MEASURES detail rows before they are mounted in the visible layout.

Why: the variable-height virtual scroll engine needs to know the exact pixel height of an expanded detail row BEFORE it appears in the visible body, otherwise totalRowsHeight jumps when the ResizeObserver lands the real height, the browser snaps the scroll thumb to the new total, and the user sees a scrollbar bounce.

How:

  1. The user clicks expand → ExpandableRowEngine.toggleRow adds the row id to state.pendingExpansion.
  2. This zone reactively renders an <ad-grid-detail-row> for each pending id, in a visibility:hidden; transform:translateX(-100000px) wrapper (off-screen, same width as the viewport so the detail row measures at its real size).
  3. The detail row's ResizeObserver fires after layout, the row emits (measured), which the zone forwards to ExpandableRowEngine.promoteFromPending — expandedRowIds now includes the id, so the visible body template mounts the detail row WITH its measured height already cached in the engine.
  4. The hidden row is removed because the id is no longer in pendingExpansion. The visible row inherits the measurement, so no second ResizeObserver tick is needed to settle the layout.

Concurrent expansions just fan out: N pending ids → N hidden detail rows → N independent ResizeObserver callbacks → N independent promoteFromPending calls.
components.rawdescription:
Hidden off-screen zone that PRE-MEASURES detail rows before they are
mounted in the visible layout.

Why: the variable-height virtual scroll engine needs to know the
exact pixel height of an expanded detail row BEFORE it appears in
the visible body, otherwise `totalRowsHeight` jumps when the
ResizeObserver lands the real height, the browser snaps the scroll
thumb to the new total, and the user sees a scrollbar bounce.

How:
 1. The user clicks expand → `ExpandableRowEngine.toggleRow` adds the
    row id to `state.pendingExpansion`.
 2. This zone reactively renders an `<ad-grid-detail-row>` for each
    pending id, in a `visibility:hidden; transform:translateX(-100000px)`
    wrapper (off-screen, same width as the viewport so the detail row
    measures at its real size).
 3. The detail row's `ResizeObserver` fires after layout, the row
    emits `(measured)`, which the zone forwards to
    `ExpandableRowEngine.promoteFromPending` — `expandedRowIds` now
    includes the id, so the visible body template mounts the detail
    row WITH its measured height already cached in the engine.
 4. The hidden row is removed because the id is no longer in
    `pendingExpansion`. The visible row inherits the measurement, so
    no second ResizeObserver tick is needed to settle the layout.

Concurrent expansions just fan out: N pending ids → N hidden detail
rows → N independent ResizeObserver callbacks → N independent
`promoteFromPending` calls.

variables.name: FORMULA_REF_PALETTE_SIZE
variables.rawdescription:
Cyclic color palette used to highlight formula references inside the
editor + the grid. Colors are *not* hard-coded — the implementation
returns a CSS custom-property reference (`var(--ad-grid-ref-color-N)`),
letting consumers rebrand the palette from their own stylesheet without
a public component input.

Assignment is *stable for the lifetime of an edit session*: the same
normalised ref text (e.g. `"A1"`, `"A1:B3"`) always receives the same
palette slot, so a cell keeps its colour while the user edits the
formula around it. The assignment resets when the editor closes.

functions.name: a1ToLongForm
functions.description:
Convert an A1-form source string to REF long-form storage. Refs that point to unknown columns are replaced with a literal #REF! to match Excel's convention for broken refs.

When ctx.currentRowId is known, relative refs pointing at the current row (=B5 on row 5, neither $ lock set) collapse to same-row storage (REF(COLUMN("field")) with no ROW arg). This preserves the "one formula covers every row" property across an edit → commit round-trip when the user only views / tweaks the formula without changing rows.

a1ToLongForm('=A1+B2', ctx) // no currentRowId → '=REF(COLUMN("price"),ROW(1))+REF(COLUMN("qty"),ROW(2))' a1ToLongForm('=A1*B1', { ...ctx, currentRowId: 'r_1' }) → '=REF(COLUMN("price"))REF(COLUMN("qty"))' // collapsed to same-row a1ToLongForm('=[price][qty]', ctx) → '=REF(COLUMN("price"))*REF(COLUMN("qty"))'

functions.name: addressToA1
functions.description: (addr) → "A1" using the current column order.

functions.name: arityAllowsErrorShortCircuit
functions.description: Built-in functions that expect to see error values as arguments (so they can react to them instead of short-circuiting). IFERROR is the canonical case. Everything else follows Excel's "first error wins" rule.

functions.name: autocompletePrefixAtCaret
functions.description: Detect the function-name autocomplete trigger: the caret sits at the end of an ident/fn/bool token whose text is non-empty.

functions.name: cellRefToA1
functions.description: Render a structured ref as A1 notation using the column order from the current grid context. Returns #REF! when the field is not visible. Same-row refs render as [field] surface shorthand.

functions.name: cellRefToLongForm
functions.description: Render a structured ref as the long-form storage string. Same-row refs omit the ROW argument entirely (REF(COLUMN("field"))), which is the signal consumed by the parser and the ref-mapper to resolve against the host cell's row at evaluation time.

functions.name: coerceDraftValue
functions.description: Coerce a raw formula-bar draft (always a string) back to the column's natural type. Empty → '', numeric literal → number (when the old cell was numeric), booleans → boolean, otherwise the string is kept verbatim. Matches the inline editor's commit-time coercion closely enough that downstream consumers see a consistent type.

functions.name: coerceJsValue
functions.description: Coerce a plain JS value from a row object into a FormulaValue.

functions.name: collectDeps
functions.description: Collect every long-form dependency referenced by the AST.

functions.name: columnIndexToLetters
functions.description: Convert a 0-based column index to its A1 letter sequence. 0 → A, 25 → Z, 26 → AA, 27 → AB, 701 → ZZ, 702 → AAA, …

functions.name: columnLettersToIndex
functions.description: Convert an A1 column-letter sequence to its 0-based index. Returns -1 for invalid input (empty string, non-letters).

functions.name: compareValues
functions.description:
Excel comparison rules (simplified):

  • Numbers compare numerically, ties broken as equal.
  • Strings compare case-insensitively, lexicographically.
  • Booleans: FALSE < TRUE.
  • Cross-type comparison orders are: number < string < boolean.
  • empty coerces to 0 / "" / FALSE depending on the other side.

Returns null when comparison is meaningless (e.g. against an error, though we already filter those out beforehand).

functions.name: cssEscape
functions.description: CSS-escape a value for use in attribute selectors.

functions.name: evaluate
functions.description: Evaluate a parsed formula. Never throws: every internal exception is converted to a #VALUE! / #REF! / #NAME? value so the caller can safely store the result without wrapping in try/catch.

functions.name: extractEditorRefTokens
functions.description: Group adjacent ref colon ref patterns into range tokens while keeping simple refs as single-cell entries. Whitespace between the components is not tolerated (Excel rule).

functions.name: fakeServerFilter
functions.description: Évaluation côté story des conditions pour simuler ce que ferait le serveur. Implémente les opérateurs courants — suffisant pour la démo.

functions.name: firstError
functions.description: Returns the first error found in the given values, or null.

functions.name: formatField
functions.description: Quote a field name when it contains characters outside the bare set.

functions.name: formatFormulaNumber
functions.description: Trim IEEE-754 float artefacts (e.g. 89.6999999999999 → 89.7) by re-rounding to 12 significant digits — the toPrecision / Number round-trip drops the noisy tail while keeping every "real" digit a user might have typed. Matches spreadsheet display conventions.

functions.name: formatRefLongFormWithLocks
functions.description: Serialize a single ref to its long-form storage representation. Emits the optional lock marker only when at least one dimension is absolute.

functions.name: formulaValueToDisplay
functions.description: Format a FormulaValue for display in a cell.

functions.name: formulaValueToJs
functions.description: Convert an evaluated FormulaValue to a plain JS value suitable for filters, sort comparators and exports. Errors map to their code string ("#DIV/0!") so text filters still see the cell as a legitimate value.

functions.name: generateConditionId
functions.description: Small helper for generating condition ids without pulling in a uuid dep.

functions.name: isConditionComplete
functions.description: True when a condition has enough information to participate in evaluation.

functions.name: isIdentLikeContext
functions.description: An A1 match A1 inside foo1 or A1bar or after _ must NOT be rewritten — it belongs to a larger identifier. Returns true when the preceding or following character would make the match part of an ident.

functions.name: longFormToA1
functions.description:
Convert REF long-form storage to A1 surface for display. Tolerant — unknown fields and out-of-range rows become #REF! in the output.

Same-row storage (REF(COLUMN("field")), no ROW) renders as a concrete A1 ref tied to the host row when ctx.currentRowId is provided — this matches spreadsheet intuition (a cell on row 5 shows =C5*D5, a cell on row 6 shows =C6*D6). Without a host row (tests, offline tools), it falls back to the [field] bracket shorthand that round-trips losslessly.

longFormToA1('=REF(COLUMN("price"),ROW(1))+1', ctx) → '=A1+1' longFormToA1('=REF(COLUMN("price"))REF(COLUMN("qty"))', ctxRow1) → '=A1B1' // with currentRowId → row 1 longFormToA1('=REF(COLUMN("price"))REF(COLUMN("qty"))', ctx) → '=[price][qty]' // no currentRowId → fallback

functions.name: normalizePinned
functions.description:
Map the Vue-flavoured aliases onto Angular's canonical pinned values:

  • 'left' → 'start'
  • 'right' → 'end'
  • everything else passes through unchanged.

Done at the directive boundary so the state manager, layout engines, and column-resize/drag features keep their 'start' | 'end' | null type narrowing intact.

functions.name: normalizePinned
functions.description: Map the Vue-flavoured aliases ('left' | 'right') onto Angular's canonical 'start' | 'end' pinned values. Other values pass through.

functions.name: paletteColorVar

functions.name: parseFormula
functions.description: Parse a formula source string into an AST. Accepts either form with or without a leading =. Throws FormulaParseError on malformed input — callers should translate that into a #PARSE! error value.

functions.name: quoteFieldForColumn
functions.description: Produce the field-quoting used inside COLUMN("…") — exposed so the editor can build REF strings when inserting click-to-pick refs.

functions.name: rangeToAddresses
functions.description: Expand a range ref into a flat, row-major list of addresses. Out-of-range coordinates yield undefined entries so the evaluator can precisely propagate #REF! per cell rather than blanket-failing the whole range.

functions.name: replaceOutsideStrings
functions.description: Walk source and only apply fn to stretches that are not inside a "…" string literal (which may appear in function args like CONCAT("A1 literal", …)). Preserves "" as an escaped quote.

functions.name: resolveAst
functions.description: Walk a parsed AST and attach resolved addresses to every ref and range node. The returned tree is a new structure — input nodes are not mutated, which keeps the parser's output cache safe to reuse.

functions.name: scanQuotedString
functions.description: Consume a "…" literal, treating "" as an escaped quote.

functions.name: structuredRefToAddress
functions.description: Convert a structured ref to a stable CellAddress.

functions.name: suggestionInsertionText
functions.description: Build the text to insert when accepting a function suggestion — always NAME( so the user can immediately start typing the first argument.

functions.name: toBoolean
functions.description: Narrow a value to a boolean, following Excel coercion rules.

functions.name: tokenAtCaret
functions.description: Token whose [start, end] range contains caret. When the caret sits exactly between two tokens, the one ending at the caret wins — this is what the autocomplete needs to match an identifier the user just typed.

functions.name: toNumber
functions.description: Narrow a value to a number, following Excel coercion rules.

functions.name: toStringValue
functions.description: Narrow a value to a string, following Excel coercion rules.

functions.name: tryScanA1
functions.description: Try to scan an A1 cell reference starting at source[pos]. Returns null when the position does not start a ref. The scan is strict: letters MUST be followed by at least one digit (possibly after a $) — a bare A is an ident, not a ref.

typealiases.name: FormulaValue

typealiases.name: GridEmptyKind
typealiases.description:
Discriminator for the empty-state context.

  • no-data : the source dataset is empty (no rows ever loaded).
  • no-results : the dataset is non-empty but the active filter / search produced zero rows.

A template registered without an explicit kind defaults to no-data and is also used as the fallback when no-results has no dedicated template.

typealiases.name: NodeKey
typealiases.description:
Dependency graph for formula cells.

Each node is identified by a stable string key (the engine uses rowId|field). Edges go from a formula cell to every cell it references — so reading the graph "forward" yields dependencies ("what do I need?"), and "reverse" yields dependents ("who needs me?").

A = B + C ▶ forward(A) = {B, C}, reverse(B) ⊇ {A}

The DAG also exposes:

  • detectCycle(from) — returns the cycle membership if the node participates in one, to be marked as #CYCLE! by the engine.
  • topoFrom(roots) — topological order of descendants of roots, used to re-evaluate only the impacted slice after a mutation.

The implementation is deliberately pure (no Angular DI) so it can be unit-tested and reused outside the grid context.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: FORMULA_REF_PALETTE_SIZE
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.rawdescription:
Cyclic color palette used to highlight formula references inside the
editor + the grid. Colors are *not* hard-coded — the implementation
returns a CSS custom-property reference (`var(--ad-grid-ref-color-N)`),
letting consumers rebrand the palette from their own stylesheet without
a public component input.

Assignment is *stable for the lifetime of an edit session*: the same
normalised ref text (e.g. `"A1"`, `"A1:B3"`) always receives the same
palette slot, so a cell keeps its colour while the user edits the
formula around it. The assignment resets when the editor closes.
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description:
Cyclic color palette used to highlight formula references inside the editor + the grid. Colors are not hard-coded — the implementation returns a CSS custom-property reference (var(--ad-grid-ref-color-N)), letting consumers rebrand the palette from their own stylesheet without a public component input.

Assignment is stable for the lifetime of an edit session: the same normalised ref text (e.g. "A1", "A1:B3") always receives the same palette slot, so a cell keeps its colour while the user edits the formula around it. The assignment resets when the editor closes.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: a1ToLongForm
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description:
Convert an A1-form source string to REF long-form storage. Refs that point to unknown columns are replaced with a literal #REF! to match Excel's convention for broken refs.

When ctx.currentRowId is known, relative refs pointing at the current row (=B5 on row 5, neither $ lock set) collapse to same-row storage (REF(COLUMN("field")) with no ROW arg). This preserves the "one formula covers every row" property across an edit → commit round-trip when the user only views / tweaks the formula without changing rows.

a1ToLongForm('=A1+B2', ctx) // no currentRowId → '=REF(COLUMN("price"),ROW(1))+REF(COLUMN("qty"),ROW(2))' a1ToLongForm('=A1*B1', { ...ctx, currentRowId: 'r_1' }) → '=REF(COLUMN("price"))REF(COLUMN("qty"))' // collapsed to same-row a1ToLongForm('=[price][qty]', ctx) → '=REF(COLUMN("price"))*REF(COLUMN("qty"))'

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: addressToA1
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: (addr) → "A1" using the current column order.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: formatRefLongFormWithLocks
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Serialize a single ref to its long-form storage representation. Emits the optional lock marker only when at least one dimension is absolute.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: isIdentLikeContext
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: An A1 match A1 inside foo1 or A1bar or after _ must NOT be rewritten — it belongs to a larger identifier. Returns true when the preceding or following character would make the match part of an ident.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: longFormToA1
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description:
Convert REF long-form storage to A1 surface for display. Tolerant — unknown fields and out-of-range rows become #REF! in the output.

Same-row storage (REF(COLUMN("field")), no ROW) renders as a concrete A1 ref tied to the host row when ctx.currentRowId is provided — this matches spreadsheet intuition (a cell on row 5 shows =C5*D5, a cell on row 6 shows =C6*D6). Without a host row (tests, offline tools), it falls back to the [field] bracket shorthand that round-trips losslessly.

longFormToA1('=REF(COLUMN("price"),ROW(1))+1', ctx) → '=A1+1' longFormToA1('=REF(COLUMN("price"))REF(COLUMN("qty"))', ctxRow1) → '=A1B1' // with currentRowId → row 1 longFormToA1('=REF(COLUMN("price"))REF(COLUMN("qty"))', ctx) → '=[price][qty]' // no currentRowId → fallback

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: quoteFieldForColumn
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Produce the field-quoting used inside COLUMN("…") — exposed so the editor can build REF strings when inserting click-to-pick refs.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: rangeToAddresses
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Expand a range ref into a flat, row-major list of addresses. Out-of-range coordinates yield undefined entries so the evaluator can precisely propagate #REF! per cell rather than blanket-failing the whole range.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: replaceOutsideStrings
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Walk source and only apply fn to stretches that are not inside a "…" string literal (which may appear in function args like CONCAT("A1 literal", …)). Preserves "" as an escaped quote.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: resolveAst
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Walk a parsed AST and attach resolved addresses to every ref and range node. The returned tree is a new structure — input nodes are not mutated, which keeps the parser's output cache safe to reuse.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: structuredRefToAddress
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Convert a structured ref to a stable CellAddress.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: arityAllowsErrorShortCircuit
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Built-in functions that expect to see error values as arguments (so they can react to them instead of short-circuiting). IFERROR is the canonical case. Everything else follows Excel's "first error wins" rule.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: compareValues
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description:
Excel comparison rules (simplified):

  • Numbers compare numerically, ties broken as equal.
  • Strings compare case-insensitively, lexicographically.
  • Booleans: FALSE < TRUE.
  • Cross-type comparison orders are: number < string < boolean.
  • empty coerces to 0 / "" / FALSE depending on the other side.

Returns null when comparison is meaningless (e.g. against an error, though we already filter those out beforehand).

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: evaluate
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Evaluate a parsed formula. Never throws: every internal exception is converted to a #VALUE! / #REF! / #NAME? value so the caller can safely store the result without wrapping in try/catch.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: autocompletePrefixAtCaret
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Detect the function-name autocomplete trigger: the caret sits at the end of an ident/fn/bool token whose text is non-empty.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: extractEditorRefTokens
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Group adjacent ref colon ref patterns into range tokens while keeping simple refs as single-cell entries. Whitespace between the components is not tolerated (Excel rule).

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: tokenAtCaret
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Token whose [start, end] range contains caret. When the caret sits exactly between two tokens, the one ending at the caret wins — this is what the autocomplete needs to match an identifier the user just typed.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: tryScanA1
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Try to scan an A1 cell reference starting at source[pos]. Returns null when the position does not start a ref. The scan is strict: letters MUST be followed by at least one digit (possibly after a $) — a bare A is an ident, not a ref.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: cellRefToA1
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Render a structured ref as A1 notation using the column order from the current grid context. Returns #REF! when the field is not visible. Same-row refs render as [field] surface shorthand.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: cellRefToLongForm
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Render a structured ref as the long-form storage string. Same-row refs omit the ROW argument entirely (REF(COLUMN("field"))), which is the signal consumed by the parser and the ref-mapper to resolve against the host cell's row at evaluation time.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: columnIndexToLetters
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Convert a 0-based column index to its A1 letter sequence. 0 → A, 25 → Z, 26 → AA, 27 → AB, 701 → ZZ, 702 → AAA, …

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: columnLettersToIndex
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Convert an A1 column-letter sequence to its 0-based index. Returns -1 for invalid input (empty string, non-letters).

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: formatField
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Quote a field name when it contains characters outside the bare set.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/grid..name: coerceDraftValue
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/grid..description: Coerce a raw formula-bar draft (always a string) back to the column's natural type. Empty → '', numeric literal → number (when the old cell was numeric), booleans → boolean, otherwise the string is kept verbatim. Matches the inline editor's commit-time coercion closely enough that downstream consumers see a consistent type.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: coerceJsValue
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Coerce a plain JS value from a row object into a FormulaValue.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: collectDeps
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Collect every long-form dependency referenced by the AST.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: suggestionInsertionText
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Build the text to insert when accepting a function suggestion — always NAME( so the user can immediately start typing the first argument.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: cssEscape
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: CSS-escape a value for use in attribute selectors.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/stori.name: fakeServerFilter
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/stori.description: Évaluation côté story des conditions pour simuler ce que ferait le serveur. Implémente les opérateurs courants — suffisant pour la démo.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/model.name: firstError
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/model.description: Returns the first error found in the given values, or null.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/model.name: toBoolean
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/model.description: Narrow a value to a boolean, following Excel coercion rules.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/model.name: toNumber
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/model.description: Narrow a value to a number, following Excel coercion rules.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/model.name: toStringValue
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/model.description: Narrow a value to a string, following Excel coercion rules.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/compo.name: formatFormulaNumber
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/compo.description: Trim IEEE-754 float artefacts (e.g. 89.6999999999999 → 89.7) by re-rounding to 12 significant digits — the toPrecision / Number round-trip drops the noisy tail while keeping every "real" digit a user might have typed. Matches spreadsheet display conventions.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/compo.name: formulaValueToDisplay
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/compo.description: Format a FormulaValue for display in a cell.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/compo.name: formulaValueToJs
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/compo.description: Convert an evaluated FormulaValue to a plain JS value suitable for filters, sort comparators and exports. Errors map to their code string ("#DIV/0!") so text filters still see the cell as a legitimate value.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/model.name: generateConditionId
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/model.description: Small helper for generating condition ids without pulling in a uuid dep.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/model.name: isConditionComplete

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: parseFormula
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description: Parse a formula source string into an AST. Accepts either form with or without a leading =. Throws FormulaParseError on malformed input — callers should translate that into a #PARSE! error value.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: scanQuotedString

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/direc.name: normalizePinned
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/direc.description:
Map the Vue-flavoured aliases onto Angular's canonical pinned values:

  • 'left' → 'start'
  • 'right' → 'end'
  • everything else passes through unchanged.

Done at the directive boundary so the state manager, layout engines, and column-resize/drag features keep their 'start' | 'end' | null type narrowing intact.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/state.name: normalizePinned

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/direc.name: GridEmptyKind
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/direc.description:
Discriminator for the empty-state context.

  • no-data : the source dataset is empty (no rows ever loaded).
  • no-results : the dataset is non-empty but the active filter / search produced zero rows.

A template registered without an explicit kind defaults to no-data and is also used as the fallback when no-results has no dedicated template.

projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.name: NodeKey
projects/grid-angular/src/lib/grid/featu.description:
Dependency graph for formula cells.

Each node is identified by a stable string key (the engine uses rowId|field). Edges go from a formula cell to every cell it references — so reading the graph "forward" yields dependencies ("what do I need?"), and "reverse" yields dependents ("who needs me?").

A = B + C ▶ forward(A) = {B, C}, reverse(B) ⊇ {A}

The DAG also exposes:

  • detectCycle(from) — returns the cycle membership if the node participates in one, to be marked as #CYCLE! by the engine.
  • topoFrom(roots) — topological order of descendants of roots, used to re-evaluate only the impacted slice after a mutation.

The implementation is deliberately pure (no Angular DI) so it can be unit-tested and reused outside the grid context.
